content:
# Wormholes

**Summary**: Small, continuous tubes formed in carbonate rocks by acid dissolution.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] investigated the sustainability of wormholes under high confining stress.

## Scientific Context

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] Wormholes are a primary mechanism for enhancing permeability during matrix acidizing.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] Visualized via CT scan and modeled using FEM.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Wormhole branches were found to be more susceptible to deformation and closure in chalk samples compared to limestone. ([[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023, p. 13]])

## Contrasting Values and Interpretations

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] Sizes ranging from 2 to 5 mm.

## Open Questions and Research Gaps
